intro: Neuontobotrys linifolius, also known as Chilean rosemary or Linaria, is a plant species within the family Boraginaceae, characterized by its narrow, linear leaves and small, blue or white flowers. Traditionally, Neuontobotrys linifolius is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate digestive issues, reduce pain, and support immune function. The bioactive constituents of Neuontobotrys linifolius include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Neuontobotrys linifolius is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and capsules, as well as in traditional folk medicine in South America, where it is used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
